lede: A look at February 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Charles Lindbergh and Ludwig Erhard, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Facebook
events.desc: Facebook, a mainstream online social networking site, is founded by Mark Zuckerberg and Eduardo Saverin.

events.title: February 1998 Afghanistan earthquake
events.desc: The 5.9 Mw Afghanistan earthquake shakes the Takhar Province with a maximum Mercalli intensity of VII (Very strong). With 2,323 killed, and 818 injured, damage is considered extreme.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Santo Tomas Internment Camp is liberated from Japanese authority.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: Adolf Hitler appoints himself as head of the Armed Forces High Command.

events.title: Philippine–American War
events.desc: The Philippine–American War begins when four Filipino soldiers enter the "American Zone" in Manila, igniting the Battle of Manila.

events.title: John Marshall
events.desc: John Marshall is sworn in as Chief Justice of the United States.

events.title: 1797 Riobamba earthquake
events.desc: The Riobamba earthquake strikes Ecuador, causing up to 40,000 casualties.

events.title: George Washington
events.desc: George Washington is unanimously elected as the first President of the United States by the U.S. Electoral College.

events.title: 1169 Sicily earthquake
events.desc: A strong earthquake strikes the Ionian coast of Sicily, causing tens of thousands of injuries and deaths, especially in Catania.

events.title: Emperor Taizu of Song
events.desc: Zhao Kuangyin declares himself Emperor Taizu of Song, ending the Later Zhou and beginning the Song dynasty.

births.name: Ludwig Erhard
births.line: German soldier and politician, second Chancellor of West Germany

births.name: Friedrich Ebert
births.line: German lawyer and politician, first President of Germany

births.name: Dan Quayle
births.line: American sergeant, lawyer, and politician, 44th Vice President of the United States

deaths.text: Matti Nykänen, Finnish Olympic-winning ski jumper and singer
Satyendra Nath Bose, Indian physicist, mathematician, and academic
Hendrik Lorentz, Dutch physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate
Charles Marie de La Condamine, French mathematician and geographer
